title: 10 Herbal Teas to Help Alleviate Gum Infection Symptoms
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms and promote healing of gum infection, also known as periodontal disease. They help reduce inflammation, fight off bacteria, and soothe pain and discomfort. The best herbal teas for gum infection include Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Salvia officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Melissa officinalis, Hydrastis canadensis, and Zingiber officinale. If left untreated, gum infection can lead to tooth loss, bad breath, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for a long time to help heal gum infections. The main reason is that it has strong antibacterial properties. When you drink tea tree oil in tea form, it helps kill the bacteria that cause gum infections. This is especially useful for people who have periodontitis, a serious gum disease. Tea tree oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduces swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, it has antifungal properties, which helps prevent fungal growth in the mouth, a common cause of gum infections. When you drink tea tree oil tea, it helps to soothe and calm the gums, making it easier for them to heal. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of tea tree oil tea also help to prevent the spread of infection, making it an effective natural remedy for gum infections.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for gum infection because they have antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria causing the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums.
Catechins are good for gum infection because they have antibacterial properties that help to reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria that cause infection and swelling in the gums.

Quercetin is good for gum infection because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight bacteria that cause infection, promoting healing and soothing the affected area.
Polyphenols are good for gum infection because they have antibacterial properties that help fight the bacteria that cause gum disease, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums.

remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal teas for gum infection
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal tea that helps with gum infection, start by gathering a handful of dried chamomile flowers, known as Chamomilla recutita. Rinse the flowers gently with clean water, then place them in a saucepan with one quart of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chamomile's healing properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers, and add honey or lemon to taste. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal gum infections. You can also store the tea in the refrigerator for later use or make a larger batch and freeze it in ice cube trays for convenient servings.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a popular remedy for gum infections. The tea is made from the root of the Echinacea plant, which has been used for centuries to boost the immune system. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, it helps to reduce inflammation in your gums, making it easier for your body to fight off the infection. The tea also has antibacterial properties, which help to kill the bacteria that cause gum infections. This can help to prevent the infection from spreading and reduce pain and discomfort in your mouth. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in your gums, making it a natural and effective remedy for gum infections. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly can also help to prevent gum infections from happening in the first place, by keeping your gums healthy and strong.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for gum infection because they have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling, prevent bacteria growth, and promote healing in the affected area.

Flavonoids are good for gum infection because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight bacteria in the infected area, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
Tannins are good for gum infection because they have antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria causing inflammation and pain in the gums, reducing swelling and promoting healing.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for gum infection
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal tea that helps with gum infection, start by gathering 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers. If you don't have dried chamomile, you can use 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers. Next, boil a cup of water in a kettle or on the stove. Once the water is boiling, add the chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth. If you don't have a tea infuser, you can simply add the flowers to the boiling water. Allow the flowers to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release their active ingredients into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the flowers from the water and discard them. Your chamomile tea is now ready to drink. To use it for gum infection, you can drink it 2-3 times a day, or use it as a mouthwash by swishing the tea in your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out. It's essential to note that while chamomile tea may help soothe gum infections, it's not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult a dentist or doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a popular remedy for gum infections because of its antibacterial properties. The tea contains compounds like eucalyptol, which kills bacteria and other germs that cause infections. When you drink eucalyptus tea, the eucalyptol enters your body and goes to your mouth, where it targets the bacteria causing the gum infection. The tea also reduces inflammation and swelling in the gums, which can be painful and make it hard to eat or speak. The antibacterial properties of eucalyptus globulus tea help to heal the gum infection by killing the bacteria and preventing it from spreading. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage the condition.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for gum infection because they have strong antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight the bacteria that cause gum disease, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
Quercetin is good for gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria causing the infection.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for gum infection
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal tea that helps with gum infection, you'll need dried chamomile flowers and a few simple items. First, boil some water and let it cool down for a minute or two, as boiling water can damage the delicate herbs. Next, add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to a cup and pour the cooled water over them. Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their healing properties. After the steeping time, strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer to catch the loose flowers. Discard the solids and your tea is ready to drink. To use it for gum infection, you can drink it warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day. You can also add a little honey to sweeten the taste, but make sure to consult with a healthcare professional or a dentist before using this tea as a treatment for gum infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tea, also known as sage tea, has been used for centuries to help soothe and heal gum infections. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have antibacterial properties that help fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections. These compounds also reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it easier for the body to heal. Drinking sage tea may also help to reduce bad breath and gum bleeding. Additionally, sage tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with gum infections. The antimicrobial properties in sage tea can also help to prevent the growth of new bacteria, reducing the risk of the infection spreading.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal tea that helps with gum infection, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers, which are rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop and then remove it from the heat. Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or directly to the boiling water, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser or by pouring the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve lined with a cheesecloth or a clean cotton cloth. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking it. You can also add a pinch of salt or a squeeze of fresh lemon juice to enhance the flavor and benefits of the tea. To treat gum infection, drink the tea 2-3 times a day, or as needed, and also practice good oral hygiene by brushing and flossing regularly, as well as visiting your dentist for regular check-ups.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol tea is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to treat gum infections. The tea is made from the resin of the Commiphora tree, which contains compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed, these compounds help to fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as gingivitis and periodontitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of Commiphora molmol tea also help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to clean and maintain good oral hygiene. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to prevent gum infections from getting worse and reduce the risk of more serious oral health problems.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a good remedy for gum infection. When you drink ginger tea, the gingerol is absorbed into your body and directly targets the infected gums, reducing the pain and discomfort caused by the infection. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to reduce the redness and swelling of the gums, making it easier to clean and care for them. remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint tea, is a natural remedy that can help with gum infections. When you drink peppermint tea, the menthol helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, making it easier to clean and care for them. The tea also helps to freshen breath and reduce bad odors, which can be a symptom of a gum infection. Additionally, peppermint tea can help to ease pain and discomfort caused by a gum infection. Peppermint tea can also help to prevent the buildup of plaque and tartar on teeth, which can contribute to gum infections. When you drink peppermint tea regularly, you can help to keep your gums and teeth healthy and strong.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita tea, also known as German chamomile, is a natural remedy that can help with gum infections. The tea contains powerful compounds called flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to heal from infections. The flavonoids also help to fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can help to calm the nerves and reduce stress, which can exacerbate gum infections. When consumed regularly, Chamomilla recutita tea may help to prevent gum infections by reducing plaque buildup and promoting healthy gums. The tea is also easy to make and consume, making it a convenient natural remedy for gum infections.

remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal teas for gum infection
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal tea that helps with gum infection, start by gathering dried chamomile flowers, which are available in most health food stores or online. Begin by boiling 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stove, and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. Let the tea cool slightly before drinking it. It is recommended to drink 1 cup of chamomile tea, 2-3 times a day, for best results. As you drink the tea, hold it in your mouth for a few seconds to allow the antibacterial properties of the chamomile to reach your gums and help reduce inflammation and fight infection.

supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal teas to remedy gum infection is a blend of Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree), Eucalyptus globulus (eucalyptus), and Zingiber officinale (ginger).  Melaleuca alternifolia has antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria that cause gum infection. Eucalyptus globulus has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Zingiber officinale has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce swelling and kill bacteria, while also providing relief from pain and discomfort.  This combination is effective because it targets the root cause of the gum infection (bacteria) and its symptoms (inflammation and pain). The antibacterial properties of Melaleuca and Zingiber work together to kill the bacteria, while the anti-inflammatory properties of Eucalyptus globulus ease the pain and swelling. This combination is also gentle on the gums, making it a suitable remedy for gum infection.  To use this combination, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia, 1 teaspoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus, and 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and drink 2-3 times a day to help remedy gum infection.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a gum infection, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can make the infection worse or slow down healing. One such tea is peppermint tea, as its cooling effect can numb the pain and make you less aware of the infection, delaying treatment. Another tea to avoid is ginger tea, which can increase blood flow and make the infection spread faster. Sage tea can also be problematic, as its antibacterial properties can be too strong for the mouth and cause more damage. Licorice root tea should be avoided as well, as it can cause an overgrowth of bacteria in the mouth and make the infection worse. Lastly, licorice root tea can also interact with medications used to treat gum infections, reducing their effectiveness.
supplementary_other_remedies: For gum infections, people can try using saltwater mouthwash. This is because salt has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing the infection. When saltwater is swished around the mouth, it can reach the infected area and help reduce swelling and pain.  Another natural remedy is oil pulling with coconut oil. This involves swishing the oil around the mouth for a few minutes before spitting it out. The oil can help reduce bacteria and inflammation in the gums, and coconut oil has antifungal properties that can help prevent further infections.  Some people also use activated charcoal powder as a natural mouthwash. This powder can help absorb bacteria and toxins from the mouth, reducing the risk of infection. When mixed with water, it can be swished around the mouth before being spat out.  People can also try using hydrogen peroxide mouthwash to help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums. This can be a more effective option than saltwater, but it's essential to use it in moderation and follow the instructions carefully to avoid damaging the gums.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with gum infections, also known as periodontitis, often experience other health issues. These include heart problems, as the bacteria in the mouth can enter the bloodstream and travel to the heart, causing inflammation. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this inflammation.  Some people with gum infections may also experience respiratory problems, such as bronchitis or pneumonia, as the bacteria can spread to the lungs. Herbs like eucalyptus and thyme have antimicrobial properties that can help combat these infections.  Gum infections can also lead to digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), as the bacteria can enter the digestive system. Herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract.  In addition, people with gum infections may experience skin problems, such as acne or eczema, as the bacteria can enter the bloodstream and cause inflammation. Herbs like aloe vera and tea tree oil have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat infections.  Finally, some people with gum infections may experience joint pain and arthritis, as the inflammation in the mouth can spread to the joints. Herbs like willow bark and meadowsweet have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
